seoTitle: How to Create Jira Reports Step by Step
metaDescription: Learn how to open the Reports interface in Jira, choose the right chart type, apply filters, and export or share your finished report.
intro: Guessing at team velocity instead of tracking it properly makes it hard to spot bottlenecks before they slow down delivery. This guide covers how to open Jira's Reports interface, select the right chart for what you're tracking, and share the results.

steps.heading: Open the Reports interface
steps.body: Navigate to the left sidebar menu in your software workspace and select the Reports interface.

steps.heading: Choose your metric grouping
steps.body: Scroll through the reporting hub and pick the metric grouping you need, such as sprint tracking or issue progress.

steps.heading: Select a specific report
steps.body: Click on the Burndown Chart or Velocity Report icon to open the graphic configuration pane for that chart type.

steps.heading: Apply filters
steps.body: Use the dropdown menus to choose your active board, target sprints, or estimation units so the chart reflects the right scope.

steps.heading: Export or share the report
steps.body: Use the header actions to export a CSV file of the data or embed the graphic onto a shared dashboard.

faq.question: Where do I find the Reports section in Jira?
faq.answer: Open your software workspace and select the Reports interface from the left sidebar menu.

faq.question: What reports are available in Jira?
faq.answer: Jira offers reports like Burndown Charts, Velocity Reports, and Cumulative Flow diagrams, depending on your metric grouping selection.

faq.question: Can I export a Jira report as a CSV?
faq.answer: Yes, the header actions in the report view let you export a CSV file of the underlying data.

faq.question: Can I embed a Jira report on a shared dashboard?
faq.answer: Yes, most reports can be embedded onto a shared dashboard directly from the report's action menu.
